title: Ternary Operator ⚖️
description: Write quick if/else decisions on a single line.

slides.title: The One-Line Decision ⚡
slides.content:
Sometimes writing a full 5-line `if / else` block just to assign a single variable feels too bulky.

C offers a slick shortcut called the **Ternary Operator** (`? :`). It compresses an if/else down to a single line.

slides.title: Ternary Syntax
slides.content:
The format is: `Condition ? ValueIfTrue : ValueIfFalse;`

It asks a question (?), returns the left side if Yes, or returns the right side (:) if No.

quizData.question: In the statement `int max = (a > b) ? a : b;`, what will `max` be assigned if `a` is 5 and `b` is 10?
quizData.explanation: The condition `(5 > 10)` is False. Ternary works by returning the right side of the `:` when false. So it returns `b` (10).

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Don't Overdo It

Ternary operators are great for assigning simple variables concisely. But never nest ternary operators inside other ternary operators. It becomes entirely unreadable to humans!

When logic gets complex, stick to the `if` / `else` block.
